name: tox-antitargets
description: Explainable computational toxicology linking antitarget / off-target protein binding to rodent acute toxicity (LD50). Reproduces Nikitin et al. 2025 (Pharmaceutics 17, 1573) from a public dataset of 12,654 compounds with mouse intravenous pLD50 and AutoDock Vina docking scores against the 44-protein Bowes safety pharmacology panel (hERG/KCNH2, AVPR1A, CACNA1C, KCNQ1, EDNRA, acetylcholinesterase, dopamine D2, cannabinoid CB1/CB2, muscarinic, adrenergic, opioid, ion channels, etc.). Use for: acute toxicity / LD50 analysis, antitarget interaction profiles, safety pharmacology, mechanism-of-action prediction via inverse docking and target fishing for a molecule given by SMILES or name, hERG and cardiotoxicity associations, NIH and Brenk medicinal-chemistry filtering, Butina chemical clustering, physicochemical property profiling, chemical-space t-SNE, and Spearman / Mann-Whitney structure-toxicity statistics.
